what:
OpenFn adaptor for Vtiger. The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Vtiger CRM.

Operations haven't been parsed for this adaptor yet — see the Sources tab for vendor API docs. Vendor homepage: https://www.vtiger.com.
connect:
Vtiger supports HTTP Basic auth or access-token for API access.

The credential schema asks for hostUrl (required), username (required), and accessToken (required). Auth reference: https://help.vtiger.com/article/147111526-Create-Access-Key.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Vtiger CRM.

1 trigger is catalogued (0 webhook, 1 polling), including New Contact. See the Triggers tab for the full list. Vtiger can push events to OpenFn via webhooks (docs: https://help.vtiger.com/article/157943229-Webhook), so reactive workflows are a good fit.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Kick the workflow off from a trigger on the Triggers tab (webhook-driven where supported, polling otherwise). Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Vtiger. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Vtiger jobs at volume:

- Prefer Vtiger webhooks over polling — they cut latency and rate-limit pressure.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Per-tenant base URL (https://<org>.od2.vtiger.com/restapi/v1/vtiger/default). The adaptor uses md5(accessKey) as the password.
best:
- Store Vtiger credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-vtiger to v1.3.29 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
